title: tx.origin ownership bypass in group manager
root_cause: Ownership check compares against tx.origin allowing phishing via intermediate contract.
attack_path: EOA signs/initiates call to malicious forwarder; forwarder calls privileged function; tx.origin matches owner; privileged state change executes.
impact: Unauthorized lock/unlock or membership mutation by non-owner.
mitigation: Bind checks to msg.sender with onlyOwner/RBAC; add regression test for indirect call (EOA -> contract -> target).
test_recommendation: Foundry: forwarder contract calling lock/unlock should revert when caller is not owner.

title: Stale oracle accepted due to missing heartbeat/confidence
root_cause: Price feed used without staleness or confidence validation; fallback triggers only on zero price.
attack_path: Attacker leaves price stale or injects out-of-band value; protocol uses price for borrow/liq; exploit over-borrow or forced liquidation.
impact: Inflated collateralization or unfair liquidations leading to fund loss.
mitigation: Enforce updatedAt/answeredInRound checks, heartbeat bound, deviation guard, and safe fallback rules.
test_recommendation: Inject stale round data in fork test; expect revert when updatedAt exceeds heartbeat.

title: Invariant drift in fee retention causes insolvency
root_cause: Asymmetric updates between liabilities and protocol fee balances during withdrawals/repayments.
attack_path: User sequence triggers withdraw/repay; liabilities decreased without matching fee/liquidity adjustment; pool becomes undercollateralized.
impact: Protocol insolvency and frozen exits for honest users.
mitigation: Enforce balance sheet invariant after each state transition; add invariant test for assets_on_chain + receivables >= user_claims + protocol_obligations.

source.link: ./Mitigation Audit | Folks Finance/Mitigation Audit _ Folks Finance 34929 - [Smart Contract - Critical] Accounting Discrepancy in Fee Retention Leads to Protocol Insolvency and Fund Freezing.md

test_recommendation: Property test: withdraw/repay/fetch-fee sequences never reduce solvency metric below zero.

title: Replay due to missing domain binding in permit
root_cause: Permit hash omits chainId/verifyingContract; signature valid across chains/contracts.
attack_path: Attacker replays signed permit on sibling deployment; gains allowance/spend rights without owner intent.
mitigation: Use full EIP-712 domain (name, version, chainId, verifyingContract) and recompute on fork.

source.link: ./ZeroLend/29342 - [SC - Insight] Lack of chainID validation allows reuse of sign....md

test_recommendation: Unit: same signature must fail on different chainId or contract address.

title: Callback reentrancy via ERC777 transfer into vault
root_cause: State updates occur after token transferFrom that can trigger tokensReceived callback.
attack_path: Attacker uses ERC777 token; transferFrom triggers callback; reenters deposit/withdraw to drain or desync balances.
mitigation: Apply CEI: update balances before external transfer; consider nonReentrant; block callback tokens if unsupported.
test_recommendation: Simulate ERC777 callback in Foundry: deposit should revert or remain consistent under reentry attempt.
